cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cees: noun. plural of cee, the name of the letter C.
censer: noun. An ornamental container for burning incense, especially during religious ceremonies.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
creep: verb. To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.
crepe: noun. A flat round pancake -like pastry from Lower Brittany, made with wheat.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
epee: noun. A sharp-pointed dueling sword with a bell-shaped guard, used (with the end blunted) in sport fencing.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
ers: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of er
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
peen: noun. The (often spherical) end of the head of a hammer opposite the main hammering end.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
pen: noun. An enclosure (enclosed area) used to contain domesticated animals, especially sheep or cattle.
pence: noun. plural of penny (the subunit of the pound sterling or Irish pound).
perse: noun. A very dark (almost black) purple or blue-gray colour.
pes: noun. the foot of a human
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
preen: noun. A forked tool used by clothiers for dressing cloth.
prees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pree
presence: noun. The fact or condition of being present, or of being within sight or call, or at hand.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
resee: verb. To see again or anew.
scene: noun. The location of an event that attracts attention.
scree: noun. Loose stony debris on a slope.
screen: noun. A physical divider intended to block an area from view, or provide shelter from something dangerous.
secern: verb. To separate or set apart (someone or something from other persons or things).
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seep: verb. To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
ser: noun. An old Indian unit of weight, equal to 80 tolas, or one fortieth of a maund.
sneer: verb. To raise a corner of the upper lip slightly, especially in scorn.
spencer: noun. A short double-breasted men's overcoat worn in the 18th and 19th centuries.
cns: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Asmat.
creen: verb. To complain; to whine; to moan.
crs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seychellois Creole.
esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
neper: noun. A non- SI unit of the attenuation (or sometimes gain) of field and power quantities such as electronic signals; the natural logarithm
pern: noun. Part of a spinning wheel, a conical spool onto which the thread is wound from the spindle.
prc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Parachi.
reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
reese: noun. Synonym of peanut butter cup
resene: noun. The component of rosin that does not react with alkali.
